why_winner: Angle C opens with the sharpest hook of the three — 'The AI industry's most successful product is not a model. It's the idea that AI has no cost.' — a sentence that earns a pause and directly challenges a belief the audience holds. It reframes the entire conversation without needing sarcasm to land the point. A's story arc is authentic but takes too long to activate; B is direct but reads like a PSA. C is the only variant that gives the reader a belief to argue with, which drives comments.

runner_up_notes.A: The revised line 'The bill exists. It just shows up somewhere else.' from the prior round is C's structural sibling — C already makes this point more elegantly. No grafting needed.
runner_up_notes.B: Nothing in B that C doesn't do better.
